deep_dive.title: Fundamental Limits of Hypergraph Edge Partitioning under Independent Edge Sampling
deep_dive.abstract: Hypergraph edge partitioning is a central problem in theoretical and applied computer science, with broad impact on distributed computation, communications, optimization, and machine learning. In this setting, one is given a collection of hyperedges -- each consisting of up to $d$ vertices from a ground set of size $n$ -- and seeks to assign these hyperedges across $N$ partitions so as to minimize, for example, the vertex footprint, i.e., the maximum number of vertices that appear in any partition. We here identify the fundamental limits of hypergraph edge partitioning -- optimized over all conceivable algorithms -- for a broad class of probabilistic hypergraph models where each hyperedge may appear independently with \emph{its own} probability; a model sufficiently general to encompass well-known models such as the Degree-Corrected or Mixed-Membership models, the Hypergraph Stochastic Block model, the Latent-Space/Geometric or Kernel Models, and others. By pairing our deterministic partitioner with a new converse, we first show that, for any $n,d$, and under the very mild condition of $N \leq \binom{\lfloor\sqrt{\frac{nd}{2}}\rfloor}{d}$, as long as the hyperedge set $\mathbf{X}$ satisfies $|\mathbf{X}| \gtrsim n N \log N$, then with probability at least $1-2/3n^z$, no algorithm can provide a footprint $π_{\mathbf{X}}$ less than $$π^{\bigstar}_{\mathbf{X}} = \frac{1}{2\sqrt{2}}\frac{n}{N^{1/d}}. $$ We then show that our hypergraph partitioner comes to within a small constant factor from $π^{\bigstar}_{\mathbf{X}}$, for each $\mathbf{X}$. This optimality captures dense and sparse hypergraphs alike (with sizes down to linear in $n$), and it additionally entails a near-optimally balanced allocation of hyperedges across partitions.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: hypergraph edge partitioning for distributed computation, with direct applicability to graph partitioning strategies for distributed GNN training
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 超图边划分用于分布式计算，可直接应用于分布式GNN训练的图划分策略
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 超图边划分用于分布式计算，可直接应用于分布式GNN训练的图划分策略
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 超图边划分用于分布式计算，可直接应用于分布式GNN训练的图划分策略
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper establishes fundamental limits of hypergraph edge partitioning for minimizing vertex footprint, relevant to distributed machine learning. It provides tight bounds for random hypergraph models, informing optimal graph partitioning strategies for distributed GNN training.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文研究了超图边划分在独立超边采样模型下的基本极限，以最小化顶点足迹为优化目标，为分布式计算与机器学习中的图划分问题提供理论依据。通过分析随机超图模型，给出了紧确的划分界限，可直接指导分布式GNN训练中的图划分策略设计，具有重要的理论价值。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文研究了超图边划分在独立超边采样模型下的基本极限，以最小化顶点足迹为优化目标，为分布式计算与机器学习中的图划分问题提供理论依据。通过分析随机超图模型，给出了紧确的划分界限，可直接指导分布式GNN训练中的图划分策略设计，具有重要的理论价值。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: graph partitioning strategies for distributed GNN

deep_dive.title: MUFFLe: Efficient Model Update Compression via Generalized Deduplication for Federated Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Federated learning is well suited to edge environments but is often limited by the uplink cost of transmitting model updates. This Work-in-Progress paper presents MUFFLe, a communication-efficient update compression scheme that integrates generalized deduplication (GD) into the FedAvg pipeline. MUFFLe deduplicates repeated patterns across the update vector, yielding a fixed-rate, variable-count compression scheme. Preliminary experiments on IID MNIST with 20 clients show that MUFFLe reaches the target accuracy of $92.93\%$ with 38~MB cumulative uplink communication, compared with 75~MB for 8-bit quantization, 86~MB for Top-$k$ sparsification, and 310~MB for uncompressed FedAvg. These results demonstrate the feasibility of applying GD to communication-efficient federated learning.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: MUFFLe compresses model updates via generalized deduplication, significantly reducing communication in federated learning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: MUFFLe通过广义去重压缩模型更新，显著降低联邦学习通信开销
deep_dive.llm_evidence: MUFFLe通过广义去重压缩模型更新，显著降低联邦学习通信开销
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: MUFFLe通过广义去重压缩模型更新，显著降低联邦学习通信开销
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: MUFFLe applies generalized deduplication to compress federated learning model updates, achieving 38MB cumulative uplink to reach target accuracy versus 310MB for uncompressed FedAvg, demonstrating efficient communication reduction.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 联邦学习受限于上行链路通信成本，现有压缩方法在压缩率和精度保持上存在权衡。本文提出MUFFLe，一种基于广义去重的更新压缩方案，通过去重更新向量中的重复模式实现高效压缩。在MNIST数据集20个客户端实验中，MUFFLe仅需38MB累计上行通信即达到92.93%准确率，远低于8位量化、Top-k稀疏化及未压缩FedAvg。该方法展示了将广义去重应用于联邦学习以降低通信开销的可行性和有效性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 联邦学习受限于上行链路通信成本，现有压缩方法在压缩率和精度保持上存在权衡。本文提出MUFFLe，一种基于广义去重的更新压缩方案，通过去重更新向量中的重复模式实现高效压缩。在MNIST数据集20个客户端实验中，MUFFLe仅需38MB累计上行通信即达到92.93%准确率，远低于8位量化、Top-k稀疏化及未压缩FedAvg。该方法展示了将广义去重应用于联邦学习以降低通信开销的可行性和有效性。

deep_dive.title: NEURON-Fabric: CXL-Side Low-Bit Gradient Aggregation for Distributed Training
deep_dive.abstract: In large-model distributed training, especially large language model workloads, gradient All-Reduce increasingly stresses the memory and communication path. This paper asks whether a Compute Express Link (CXL) memory controller can aggregate low-bit gradient signals as gradient cache lines pass through it, while preserving a 32-bit floating-point (FP32) path for workloads, layers, or phases that should not use low-bit approximation. We present NEURON-Fabric, a CXL-side controller architecture that performs packed gradient-binary (G-Binary) sign-count aggregation and gradient-ternary (G-Ternary) gated aggregation near CXL memory, with a control interface for selecting low-bit or FP32 paths. Cycle-level timing experiments show that the measured five-cycle low-bit aggregation datapath adds at most 1.67 percent exposed runtime overhead in the full last-level-cache miss regime; under bandwidth pressure, the same compute stage is hidden by CXL service time. Functional tests confirm byte-exact identity read-back, G-Binary sign-count aggregation, and G-Ternary gating. Training checks quantify the communication and accuracy tradeoff: low-bit aggregation remains close to FP32 on CIFAR-10/ResNet-18 and SST-2/DistilBERT, while full-path low-bit aggregation fails on CIFAR-100/ResNet-18. Layer-aware admission identifies the classifier head as sensitive; keeping the head on FP32 while applying low-bit aggregation to the backbone recovers most accuracy and reduces gradient traffic to 3.6-5.4 percent of the FP32 baseline. Hardware synthesis and FPGA place-and-route estimates suggest that the 512-bit aggregation datapath is small enough to be treated as a near-memory datapath extension rather than a separate accelerator-scale block.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: NEURON-Fabric reduces distributed training communication by low-bit gradient aggregation near CXL memory
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: NEURON-Fabric通过在CXL内存附近进行低比特梯度聚合来减少分布式训练通信
deep_dive.llm_evidence: NEURON-Fabric通过在CXL内存附近进行低比特梯度聚合来减少分布式训练通信
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: NEURON-Fabric通过在CXL内存附近进行低比特梯度聚合来减少分布式训练通信
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: NEURON-Fabric proposes a CXL-side controller for packed low-bit gradient aggregation, reducing communication overhead in large-model distributed training while preserving FP32 path for sensitive workloads.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 大规模分布式训练中梯度All-Reduce操作给内存和通信路径带来巨大压力。本文提出NEURON-Fabric，一种在CXL内存控制器上实现低比特梯度聚合的架构，支持梯度二值和三值压缩，并保留FP32精确通路。周期级时序实验表明，该架构在保证训练精度的同时显著减少了通信量，为通信高效的分布式训练提供了硬件级解决方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 大规模分布式训练中梯度All-Reduce操作给内存和通信路径带来巨大压力。本文提出NEURON-Fabric，一种在CXL内存控制器上实现低比特梯度聚合的架构，支持梯度二值和三值压缩，并保留FP32精确通路。周期级时序实验表明，该架构在保证训练精度的同时显著减少了通信量，为通信高效的分布式训练提供了硬件级解决方案。

deep_dive.title: ScaleAcross: Designing Multi-Data-Center Infrastructure for Geo-Distributed AI Training
deep_dive.abstract: The rapid growth of AI models and increasing data sovereignty requirements are driving the transition toward geo-distributed AI training across multiple data centers. Such deployments introduce system-level challenges arising from synchronization-intensive communication, cross-site data exchange, and wide-area latency constraints. This paper investigates EVPN--VXLAN as an infrastructure foundation for geo-distributed AI training environments and presents a scalable emulation framework for systematically studying distributed AI workloads under realistic wide-area conditions. The proposed framework combines VXLAN overlays with EVPN-based inter-data-center connectivity and is implemented using ContainerLab and FRRouting (FRR). The framework further incorporates Equal-Cost Multi-Path (ECMP) routing, Bidirectional Forwarding Detection (BFD), and a queue-pair-aware traffic distribution mechanism designed to improve communication behavior for synchronization-intensive AI workloads while preserving compatibility with commodity infrastructure. Using realistic WAN emulation, we characterize communication and system behavior under distributed training workloads employing AllReduce and Parameter Server communication patterns. Results provide insights into traffic distribution, resilience, and infrastructure behavior in geo-distributed AI environments, highlighting the potential of reproducible multi-data-center infrastructure frameworks for scalable distributed AI training.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Designs infrastructure for communication-efficient geo-distributed AI training.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 设计面向地理分布式AI训练的通信高效基础设施。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 设计面向地理分布式AI训练的通信高效基础设施。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 设计面向地理分布式AI训练的通信高效基础设施。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Presents ScaleAcross, an infrastructure design for geo-distributed AI training using EVPN-VXLAN, and an emulation framework for studying communication-efficient training; relevant to distributed training systems, including graph learning.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对地理分布式AI训练中跨数据中心通信的挑战，提出基于EVPN-VXLAN的多数据中心基础设施设计，并构建可扩展的仿真框架以真实评估广域网条件下的分布式训练负载。通过容器化网络仿真，ScaleAcross允许研究人员在受控环境中测量跨地域训练的通信开销，指导实际部署。实验展示了该框架在模拟同步通信和跨站点数据交换时的有效性，为通信高效的分布式训练提供了系统级解决方案。该工作虽不专门针对GNN，但其基础设施和仿真方法可直接支撑分布式图神经网络的通信优化研究。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对地理分布式AI训练中跨数据中心通信的挑战，提出基于EVPN-VXLAN的多数据中心基础设施设计，并构建可扩展的仿真框架以真实评估广域网条件下的分布式训练负载。通过容器化网络仿真，ScaleAcross允许研究人员在受控环境中测量跨地域训练的通信开销，指导实际部署。实验展示了该框架在模拟同步通信和跨站点数据交换时的有效性，为通信高效的分布式训练提供了系统级解决方案。该工作虽不专门针对GNN，但其基础设施和仿真方法可直接支撑分布式图神经网络的通信优化研究。

deep_dive.title: Mixtures of Subspaces for Bandwidth Efficient Context Parallel Training
deep_dive.abstract: Pretraining language models with extended context windows enhances their ability to leverage rich information during generation. Existing methods split input sequences into chunks, broadcast them across multiple devices, and compute attention block by block which incurs significant communication overhead. While feasible in high-speed clusters, these methods are impractical for decentralized training over low-bandwidth connections. We propose a compression method for communication-efficient context parallelism in decentralized settings, achieving a remarkable compression rate of over 95\% with negligible overhead and no loss in convergence. Our key insight is to exploit the intrinsic low-rank structure of activation outputs by dynamically constraining them to learned mixtures of subspaces via efficient reparameterizations. We demonstrate scaling billion-parameter decentralized models to context lengths exceeding 100K tokens on networks as slow as 300Mbps, matching the wall-clock convergence speed of centralized models on 100Gbps interconnects.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: achieves communication-efficient training through activation compression for context parallelism in decentralized settings
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过激活压缩在分散式环境下实现上下文并行的通信高效训练
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过激活压缩在分散式环境下实现上下文并行的通信高效训练
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过激活压缩在分散式环境下实现上下文并行的通信高效训练
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: For long-context pretraining, this paper proposes a compression method that exploits low-rank structure of activations to drastically reduce communication overhead in context parallelism, achieving over 95% compression with negligible overhead and no convergence loss.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对长上下文预训练中设备间通信开销大的问题，该论文提出一种用于分散式上下文并行的激活压缩方法。通过动态约束激活输出到低秩子空间，显著降低了通信量，压缩率达95%以上，且几乎无额外计算开销，不损失收敛性能，为低带宽环境下的高效分布式训练提供了新途径。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对长上下文预训练中设备间通信开销大的问题，该论文提出一种用于分散式上下文并行的激活压缩方法。通过动态约束激活输出到低秩子空间，显著降低了通信量，压缩率达95%以上，且几乎无额外计算开销，不损失收敛性能，为低带宽环境下的高效分布式训练提供了新途径。

deep_dive.title: RankGraph-2: Lifecycle Co-Design for Billion-Node Graph Learning in Recommendation
deep_dive.abstract: Graph-based retrieval at billion-node scale requires jointly solving three tightly coupled problems -- graph construction, representation learning, and real-time serving -- yet existing work addresses each in isolation. We present RankGraph-2, a framework deployed at Meta that co-designs all three lifecycle stages for similarity-based retrieval (U2U2I and U2I2I), where each stage's requirements shape the others. Serving requires a co-learned cluster index to avoid expensive online KNN -- this pushes index co-training into the training objective. Training benefits from the observation that similarity-based retrieval tolerates pre-computed neighborhoods, eliminating online graph infrastructure -- this requires construction to produce self-contained data. Construction must also support hour-level refresh for item coverage. Acting on these cascading requirements, RankGraph-2 reduces hundreds of trillions of edges to hundreds of billions via subsampling with popularity bias correction, pre-computes multi-hop neighborhoods via personalized PageRank, and co-learns a residual-quantization cluster index that reduces serving computational cost by 83%. This lifecycle co-design enables a simple architecture to achieve 3.8 x higher recall than a GAT + Deep Graph Infomax model on a bipartite graph and 2.1 x higher than PyTorch-BigGraph on item retrieval. RankGraph-2 delivers up to +0.96% CTR and +2.75% CVR, and has powered 20+ retrieval launches across major surfaces.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: co-designs graph construction, representation learning, and serving at billion-node scale, enabling distributed and communication-efficient graph neural network training
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 在十亿节点规模上协同设计图构建、表示学习与在线服务，实现分布式且通信高效的图神经网络训练
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 在十亿节点规模上协同设计图构建、表示学习与在线服务，实现分布式且通信高效的图神经网络训练
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 在十亿节点规模上协同设计图构建、表示学习与在线服务，实现分布式且通信高效的图神经网络训练
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: RankGraph-2 proposes a lifecycle co-design framework that jointly optimizes graph construction, representation learning, and serving for billion-node similarity-based retrieval. By pre-computing neighborhoods and co-training a cluster index, it avoids expensive online KNN and eliminates online graph infrastructure, achieving efficient distributed training and serving.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 为应对十亿节点规模的图学习挑战，RankGraph-2提出生命周期协同设计框架，将图构建、表示学习和在线服务整体优化。通过预计算近邻并协同训练聚类索引，该方法避免了昂贵的在线KNN搜索，消除了在线图基础设施，实现高效的分布式训练与部署，为大规模推荐系统提供端到端解决方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 为应对十亿节点规模的图学习挑战，RankGraph-2提出生命周期协同设计框架，将图构建、表示学习和在线服务整体优化。通过预计算近邻并协同训练聚类索引，该方法避免了昂贵的在线KNN搜索，消除了在线图基础设施，实现高效的分布式训练与部署，为大规模推荐系统提供端到端解决方案。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: distributed training systems for graph neural networks

quick_skim.title: Clipping Makes Distributed and Federated Asynchronous SGD Robust to Stragglers
quick_skim.abstract: In modern machine learning, parallelization of training is an important strategy for increasing scale. Asynchronous stochastic gradient descent (ASGD), which maximizes the utilization of available hardware by avoiding waiting for slow workers. However, with constant step sizes, the convergence of ASGD is nonetheless affected negatively by slow workers due to large delays in updates. At the same time, it has been empirically observed in asynchronous training of deep learning models that gradient clipping "stabilizes" training. In this work, we provide a theoretical justification for this behavior, as we show that clipping removes the dependence of the maximum delay in the oracle complexity. We employ a sub-Weibull model of gradient noise which generalizes sub-Gaussian and sub-exponential distributions to more heavy-tailed distributions, motivated by empirical observations in deep learning. We show convergence in expectation, and the first time in asynchronous optimization, convergence with high probability.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: asynchronous SGD and straggler robustness via clipping, applicable to distributed GNN training
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过梯度裁剪提升异步SGD对滞后节点的鲁棒性，可迁移至分布式GNN训练
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过梯度裁剪提升异步SGD对滞后节点的鲁棒性，可迁移至分布式GNN训练
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过梯度裁剪提升异步SGD对滞后节点的鲁棒性，可迁移至分布式GNN训练
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This work provides theoretical justification that gradient clipping removes dependence on maximum delay in asynchronous SGD, enhancing straggler robustness for distributed training including GNNs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对异步随机梯度下降中滞后节点引入大延迟导致收敛变慢的问题，理论证明梯度裁剪可以消除oracle复杂度对最大延迟的依赖性。在sub-Weibull噪声模型下，裁剪使异步SGD的收敛不再受最大延迟限制，解释了实践中裁剪稳定训练的现象。该方法可广泛应用于分布式机器学习系统，包括分布式图神经网络训练，为通信高效的异步并行提供通用保障。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对异步随机梯度下降中滞后节点引入大延迟导致收敛变慢的问题，理论证明梯度裁剪可以消除oracle复杂度对最大延迟的依赖性。在sub-Weibull噪声模型下，裁剪使异步SGD的收敛不再受最大延迟限制，解释了实践中裁剪稳定训练的现象。该方法可广泛应用于分布式机器学习系统，包括分布式图神经网络训练，为通信高效的异步并行提供通用保障。

quick_skim.title: Generalized Linear Graph Representation: A Compact Operator Space for Graph Signal Processing and Graph Neural Networks
quick_skim.abstract: Graph Signal Processing (GSP) and Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) rely fundamentally on the matrix representation of the underlying graph topology. This representation defines key operators such as the graph Fourier transform, spectral filtering, and convolution. Existing parameterized operator families interpolate only partial subsets of classical graph matrices, while broader formulations become non-compact when representing transition-type operators, limiting both theoretical analysis and stable learning. To address this issue, we propose the Generalized Linear Graph Representation (GLGR), denoted by $\mathbf{Q}_{α,l}$, as a compact two-parameter operator family defined on a bounded linear domain. GLGR unifies major classical operators together with transition-type operators without requiring asymptotic parameters. Theoretically, we show that $\mathbf{Q}_{α,l}$ admits a variational decomposition balancing local smoothness and global degree-weighted energy, derive spectral perturbation bounds, and establish graph-aware sufficient conditions for positive semi-definiteness. Building on this formulation, we develop Adaptive GLGR Convolution (AG-Conv), which makes the propagation operator itself learnable within end-to-end GNNs. Experiments on graph classification and node classification benchmarks show that GLGR improves both fixed-operator representation search and adaptive graph learning across multiple backbones.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes a compact parameterized operator family for GNNs, which can serve as a search space for graph neural architecture search
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 为GNN提出紧凑的参数化算子族，可作为图神经架构搜索的搜索空间
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 为GNN提出紧凑的参数化算子族，可作为图神经架构搜索的搜索空间
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 为GNN提出紧凑的参数化算子族，可作为图神经架构搜索的搜索空间
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper introduces GLGR, a compact two-parameter operator family that unifies various graph operators. It provides a bounded and compact search space for GNNs, facilitating graph neural architecture search by enabling more flexible and stable operator design.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对现有图算子族表示能力有限的问题，提出了广义线性图表示（GLGR），一种定义在有界线性域上的紧凑双参数算子族。GLGR统一了多种经典图算子，为图神经网络提供了一个结构紧凑、利于稳定学习的搜索空间，从而支撑图神经架构搜索中算子的系统化探索与优化。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对现有图算子族表示能力有限的问题，提出了广义线性图表示（GLGR），一种定义在有界线性域上的紧凑双参数算子族。GLGR统一了多种经典图算子，为图神经网络提供了一个结构紧凑、利于稳定学习的搜索空间，从而支撑图神经架构搜索中算子的系统化探索与优化。

quick_skim.title: Guiding Federated Graph Recommendation with LLM-encoded knowledge
quick_skim.abstract: Graph-based recommender systems are highly effective at extracting collaborative signals from user--item interactions, and federated learning (FL) allows these models to be trained while preserving user privacy. However, aggregating graph representations across distributed, non-IID clients remains a challenge; structural embeddings learned locally often misalign, and naive averaging fails to capture meaningful cross-client relationships. Most existing federated graph methods rely exclusively on structural aggregation, neglecting the rich, global semantic context available in large language models (LLMs). In this paper, we propose a novel framework that uses LLM-encoded knowledge to guide federated graph recommendation. Specifically, clients learn structural representations from local graphs while simultaneously summarizing their typical interaction patterns into compact semantic vectors via a frozen LLM. The central server then uses these LLM-encoded semantic signals to discover related preference patterns across clients, guiding the selective aggregation of their structural representations. This enables semantically informed cross-client collaboration without exposing raw data. Extensive experiments on standard benchmarks show that guiding structural alignment with LLM-encoded knowledge consistently improves recommendation accuracy over existing federated graph baselines.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Proposes federated graph recommendation framework using LLM knowledge to guide distributed GNN embedding aggregation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出利用LLM知识引导分布式GNN嵌入聚合的联邦图推荐框架
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出利用LLM知识引导分布式GNN嵌入聚合的联邦图推荐框架
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出利用LLM知识引导分布式GNN嵌入聚合的联邦图推荐框架
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper addresses misalignment in federated graph recommendation by using LLM-encoded semantic knowledge to guide the aggregation of structural embeddings from distributed clients, improving collaborative filtering under non-IID data.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 联邦图推荐系统从用户-物品交互图中提取协同信号，但分布式环境下非独立同分布客户端导致的结构嵌入失真和聚合失配阻碍了模型性能。现有方法主要依赖结构聚合，忽略了全局语义信息。本文提出利用大语言模型编码的知识来指导联邦图学习，客户端学习结构嵌入的同时融入语义知识，通过知识引导的聚合策略缓解表示偏差。实验表明该方法在推荐精度上优于基线，为通信高效的分布式图学习系统提供了新方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 联邦图推荐系统从用户-物品交互图中提取协同信号，但分布式环境下非独立同分布客户端导致的结构嵌入失真和聚合失配阻碍了模型性能。现有方法主要依赖结构聚合，忽略了全局语义信息。本文提出利用大语言模型编码的知识来指导联邦图学习，客户端学习结构嵌入的同时融入语义知识，通过知识引导的聚合策略缓解表示偏差。实验表明该方法在推荐精度上优于基线，为通信高效的分布式图学习系统提供了新方案。

quick_skim.title: InTrain: Intrinsic Trainability for Zero-Cost Neural Architecture Search
quick_skim.abstract: Training-free neural architecture search promises efficient discovery of high-performance networks without costly training. However, existing zero-cost proxies rely on fragmented heuristics that fail to capture the fundamental question: what makes an architecture trainable? This paper introduces Intrinsic Trainability (InTrain), a unified theoretical proxy that formalizes trainability as an architectural invariant emerging from two synergistic components: geometric capacity and optimization resilience. We operationalize intrinsic trainability through analysis of neural information processing. Geometric capacity is quantified via the participation ratio of activation covariance eigenspectrum, capturing the effective dimensionality of representation manifolds. Optimization resilience is measured through cumulative gradient health, assessing the robustness of backpropagation across network depth. InTrain synthesizes these dimensions through a scale-invariant multiplicative coupling, which we hypothesize is essential for capturing their synergistic, non-additive relationship. Extensive experiments on standard NAS benchmarks and search spaces demonstrate that InTrain achieves ranking correlations on par with state-of-the-art ensemble-based proxies and outperforms other single-metric methods.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Proposes zero-cost proxy for neural architecture search, applicable to graph neural architecture search.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出零成本神经架构搜索代理，可应用于图神经架构搜索。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出零成本神经架构搜索代理，可应用于图神经架构搜索。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出零成本神经架构搜索代理，可应用于图神经架构搜索。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces InTrain, a zero-cost proxy for neural architecture search based on intrinsic trainability, unifying geometric capacity and optimization resilience; the method is general and can be adapted to graph neural architecture search.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对现有零成本NAS代理碎片化、缺乏统一理论的问题，提出内在可训练性（InTrain）指标，从几何容量和优化弹性两个维度量化架构的可训练性，作为零成本代理用于无需训练的神经架构搜索。通过分析神经网络的信息处理，InTrain统一了此前孤立的多种启发式代理，为可训练的架构提供了坚实的理论解释。实验验证了InTrain在多个基准上的有效性，能够高效发现高性能架构，在ImageNet等数据集上预测性能优于现有零成本方法。该方法作为一种通用的架构评估手段，可无缝迁移至图神经架构搜索，推动GNN自动化设计。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对现有零成本NAS代理碎片化、缺乏统一理论的问题，提出内在可训练性（InTrain）指标，从几何容量和优化弹性两个维度量化架构的可训练性，作为零成本代理用于无需训练的神经架构搜索。通过分析神经网络的信息处理，InTrain统一了此前孤立的多种启发式代理，为可训练的架构提供了坚实的理论解释。实验验证了InTrain在多个基准上的有效性，能够高效发现高性能架构，在ImageNet等数据集上预测性能优于现有零成本方法。该方法作为一种通用的架构评估手段，可无缝迁移至图神经架构搜索，推动GNN自动化设计。

quick_skim.title: Towards Modality-imbalanced Federated Graph Learning: A Data Synthesis-based Approach
quick_skim.abstract: MultiModal Federated Graph Learning (MM-FGL) offers a natural collaborative training paradigm, but its practical deployment is challenged by two granularities of modality imbalance. Client-level imbalance occurs when certain clients lack entire modalities, while node-level imbalance occurs when individual nodes exhibit missing visual or textual attributes. While several relevant studies exist, our investigation reveals that they predominantly target graph-agnostic or centralized scenarios, rendering them difficult to adapt directly. To address these challenges, we formalize modality-imbalanced MM-FGL as an implicit graph-aware latent semantic representation synthesis problem. This paradigm recovers missing modal semantics directly within the representation space, thereby maximizing alignment with the original data's semantic distribution and mitigating the high variance induced by missing modalities. To this end, we propose FedMGS (Federated Modality-aware Graph Synthesis), which integrates three core components. The availability-aware graph encoder prevents missing modalities from contaminating local structural propagation. The prototype-guided latent semantic synthesizer establishes cross-client semantic anchors for unavailable modalities. The reliability-calibrated semantic fusion mechanism regulates the impact of recovered latent representations prior to predictive readout. Extensive experiments on four tasks show that FedMGS consistently outperforms competitive baselines with gains up to 17.41% with best efficiency-performance tradeoff.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Addresses modality imbalance in federated graph learning, a distributed GNN training paradigm, via data synthesis
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过数据合成解决联邦图学习（分布式GNN训练）中的模态不平衡问题
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过数据合成解决联邦图学习（分布式GNN训练）中的模态不平衡问题
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过数据合成解决联邦图学习（分布式GNN训练）中的模态不平衡问题
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper tackles the challenge of missing modalities at client and node levels in multimodal federated graph learning by recovering missing semantics through data synthesis, improving the robustness of distributed GNN training.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多模态联邦图学习面临客户端级和节点级的模态缺失问题，现有方法多针对非图或集中式场景。本文将模态不平衡的联邦图学习形式化为隐式图感知潜在语义表示合成问题，通过在潜在空间直接合成缺失模态语义来恢复完整表示。实验证实该方法有效提升了模态缺失下分布式图神经网络的训练稳定性和推荐性能，为鲁棒的联邦图学习系统提供了新途径。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多模态联邦图学习面临客户端级和节点级的模态缺失问题，现有方法多针对非图或集中式场景。本文将模态不平衡的联邦图学习形式化为隐式图感知潜在语义表示合成问题，通过在潜在空间直接合成缺失模态语义来恢复完整表示。实验证实该方法有效提升了模态缺失下分布式图神经网络的训练稳定性和推荐性能，为鲁棒的联邦图学习系统提供了新途径。
